extraction.one_line_summary: SEPKUSS Toms attacked from 17-rider breakaway on final 7.9km climb (9.7% gradient), distanced GUERREIRO and GEGGENHART in closing kilometers for maiden Grand Tour stage win.
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma placed BENNETT in break to cover SEILER's KOM hunt, sacrificingbreak numbers to control competitors' jersey ambitions.
SEPKUSS initially followed wheels conservatively mid-stage expecting GC catch, only committed full effort when gap exceeded critical threshold.
RODRIGUEZ dropped early on final climb despite teammate SEPKUSS making initial selection, exposing Euskadi-Basque team miscommunication on protected rider.
ROGLIC-VALVERDE finished together at 2:14, showing Sky/Movistar détente allows mutual GC protection against third-party attacks.

rider_signals.SEPKUSS Toms: positive: strategically conserved energy in break until gap security allowed stage commitment, showing mature race intelligence beyond gregario role
rider_signals.RODRIGUEZ Oscar: negative: dropped from winning move featuring own teammate SEPKUSS, suggesting internal team hierarchy confusion or declining form
rider_signals.BENNETT George: neutral: deployed by Jumbo solely to police SEILER's KOM ambitions rather than stage hunting, reveals team prioritizes jersey defense
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: comfortable with VALVERDE at 2:14 gap, shows red jersey control sufficient to allow gregario SEPKUSS stage freedom
rider_signals.GUERREIRO Ruben: positive: nearly matched SEPKUSS on brutal final climb despite being former teammates, confirms climbing form renaissance
rider_signals.GEGGENHART Tom: positive: third place after bridging from chase groups demonstrates EF's climbing depth beyond HIGUITA
